oai_dc:dc.dc:title: The Status of the Gentle Parrot
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waugh, David
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: In the forests of the Andes, a group of mysterious parrots in the genus Hapalopsittaca inhabit high-elevation landscapes affected by human activity. Their name combines ancient Greek words meaning gentle and parrot. This article reviews the status, habitat, conservation needs, and ecology of the Rusty-faced, Indigo-winged, Red-faced, and Black-winged parrots. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): AFA Watchbird April 2025
